title: Golden Circle to Hella
sub: Land, grab the car, and loop the Golden Circle on the way to your first base. A big but forgiving day — nothing here needs a booked time; lunch is a casual stop at Efstidalur II farm café (Friðheimar was fully booked).

stops.name: Land at Keflavík & grab your Blue car
stops.desc: Clear immigration, pick up your booked Blue rental, and download offline maps. Skip the pricey airport café — you pass a cheap supermarket this afternoon.
stops.tip: Photograph the car for existing damage before you drive off, and check the fuel policy.

stops.name: Keflavík → Þingvellir
stops.via: Loops north past Reykjavík — a Krónan in Mosfellsbær is an early cheap-grocery option.

stops.name: Þingvellir National Park
stops.desc: Walk the Almannagjá rift where the North-American and Eurasian plates pull apart, down to Öxarárfoss waterfall. UNESCO site and home of the world's oldest parliament.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,000 ISK/day, paid via the Parka app or the lot machine. Park at P1 for the rift-plus-waterfall combo; the boardwalk is flat and easy.

stops.name: Lunch at Efstidalur II farm café
stops.desc: Burgers and the farm's famous ice cream, served right over the cow barn — you can watch the cows while you eat. Casual, walk-in, no reservation needed.
stops.tip: Friðheimar was fully booked, so this is the primary lunch stop now. It's just off Rte 37 near Laugarvatn, on the way to Geysir. Parking at the farm is free.

opts.b: Worth a walk-in try among the tomato vines if you're passing by — no table is held, so it's hit or miss.
Skyr, bread, hummus, veg — eat it at Gullfoss with a view.

stops.name: Geysir & Strokkur
stops.desc: Strokkur blasts boiling water 20–30m skyward every 5–10 minutes while the whole field steams and bubbles.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,000 ISK via the Parka app — pay before you walk to the field. Stand upwind and pre-focus — it gives a little 'gulp' right before it erupts.

stops.name: Gullfoss — the Golden Falls
stops.desc: A two-tier torrent thundering into a canyon — one of Iceland's most powerful falls. Walk both the upper viewpoint and the lower path.
stops.tip: Parking is free here — one of the few big sites left that is. Midday spray often throws a rainbow. The lower path gets slippery — grippy shoes.

stops.name: Kerið crater lake
stops.desc: A 3,000-year-old volcanic crater with rust-red walls cupping a vivid blue-green lake. Easy rim loop, or walk down to the water.
stops.tip: 600 ISK parking, paid at the on-site kiosk (cash/card — no app needed). Right on Rte 35 heading south — zero detour. Late sun deepens the red rock.
stops.swap: Not jet-lagged and craving the famous blue waterfall? Brúarfoss is ~30 min north with a 45–60 min round-trip walk. Do it instead of Kerið only if you have the energy.

stops.name: Stock up at Bónus, Selfoss
stops.desc: Iceland's cheapest supermarket (pink piggy sign). Load up on skyr, bread, veg and picnic fixings for the next two days — restaurant breakfasts are steep.
stops.tip: Krónan sits next door and this is the last big fuel before the coast. Fill the tank. Supermarket parking is free.

opts.b: Iceland's most iconic chocolate wafer bar — a road-trip staple since the 1950s.
High-protein cultured dairy in every flavor — the classic Icelandic snack, grab a few for the cooler bag.
Dense, faintly sweet 'lava bread' traditionally baked underground in hot springs — great with butter.
The classic Icelandic soft drink — pairs with rúgbrauð for a proper picnic.
Nóa Síríus salted licorice/chocolate — a beloved Icelandic staple, love-it-or-hate-it.

stops.name: Dinner in Hella
stops.desc: Small town, a handful of solid casual spots — pizza, burgers and a local fish stew cover both of you without fuss.
stops.tip: Street parking around Hella is free — no app needed.

opts.b: Relaxed pizza, pasta & burgers with veggie options — the fish stew is the local specialty Tripi should try.
Cheap, quick burgers, fries & wraps in the centre.
10 min east — homemade veg/vegan + freshly caught fish, great value.

stops.name: Helja Stay Glamping Domes
stops.desc: Your booked first night — a glamping dome near Hella with mountain views. A fun, quirky start, and cosier than it looks (real beds + heating).
stops.gq: Helja Stay Glamping Domes, Hella, Iceland

title: Waterfalls, Glacier Hike & East to Höfn
sub: Your biggest day: the classic south-coast waterfalls and a glacier hike, then a scenic run east to Höfn — arriving in time to catch Vestrahorn in golden light.
route: Hella → Seljalandsfoss → Skógafoss → glacier → Höfn (~200 mi).

stops.name: Hella → Seljalandsfoss
stops.via: Leave by ~8:15am to have the falls nearly to yourselves.

stops.name: Seljalandsfoss — walk behind the falls
stops.desc: One of the few waterfalls you can walk behind — a full curtain of water around you. Early means far fewer people and easy parking.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,000 ISK via the Parka app or the lot machine — it also covers Gljúfrabúi next door. You will get misted — rain jacket + protect your camera. It faces west, so mornings are shady; the trade-off is empty paths.

stops.name: Gljúfrabúi — the hidden waterfall
stops.desc: Just 200m north of Seljalandsfoss, tucked inside a mossy canyon most visitors walk right past. Wade in to stand in the spray chamber.
stops.tip: Uses the same Seljalandsfoss parking — no extra fee. Waterproof shoes or sandals — you rock-hop a shallow creek to get in. Empty and magic at this hour.

stops.name: Skógafoss
stops.desc: A perfect 60m curtain you can walk right up to. Climb the 527 steps up the side for a top-down view and the start of the Fimmvörðuháls trail.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,000 ISK via the Parka app (the ticket's valid 8 hrs, so it covers your whole stop). Sunny mornings throw a rainbow (sometimes double) at the base — get close for the shot, then dry off.

stops.name: Early lunch before the glacier
stops.desc: Fuel up now — you won't eat again until mid-afternoon after the hike.

opts.b: Cheap quick bites right by the Skógafoss lot (seasonal).
Sit-down burgers, soup & veggie pasta with a falls view.
Yesterday's supplies + cocoa from a thermos.

stops.name: Skógafoss → Sólheimajökull
stops.via: Short gravel spur off Rte 1 to the glacier car park.

stops.name: Glacier hike on Sólheimajökull (Arctic Adventures)
stops.desc: Your booked Glacier Experience with Arctic Adventures — strap on crampons and walk onto the living Sólheimajökull glacier tongue (blue ice, crevasses and ash bands) with a certified guide. Beginner-friendly, ~3 hrs including all glacier gear.
stops.tip: Meet at the Sólheimajökull Base by ~1:30pm (tour starts 2:00pm) — no Reykjavík pickup needed. Hiking boots with ankle support are required; rent on-site if you don't have them (waterproof pants/jackets also available). Min age 8. Check your email that morning — Arctic Adventures cancels for unsafe conditions. Cancelling within 1 day = 100% fee. Parking at the glacier base is free for booked tour guests.

bk.Tour: Glacier Experience — A Glacier Hike on Sólheimajökull

stops.name: Sólheimajökull → Höfn
stops.via: Off the ice by ~5pm; you'll reach Höfn around 7:45pm. Cruise east past Vík, Eldhraun and Fjaðrárgljúfur — you'll stop at those tomorrow on the way back. Top off fuel in Vík.

stops.name: Dinner in Höfn
stops.desc: Höfn is Iceland's langoustine (mini-lobster) capital — that's shellfish, not for you two, but every spot also does solid veg & chicken. Book ahead if you can.
stops.tip: Street parking around Höfn is free.

opts.b: Harbour warehouse; seasonal veggie mains + chicken — reserve.
Value-focused; veggie pizza, burgers, big menu.
Retro diner — cheap veggie burger, fries, fish & chips.

stops.name: Vestrahorn at Stokksnes — golden hour
stops.desc: 15 min east of Höfn: jagged black peaks over black-sand dunes, one of Iceland's most photographed scenes. In late July the sun stays low and warm until ~10:45pm, so after dinner is prime time — and it's right by your bed tonight.
stops.tip: Parking + access is the ~900 ISK fee at the Viking Café — a private road toll, paid on-site, no app needed. Calm evenings mirror the peaks in the wet sand.
stops.swap: Cloudy tonight? It's just as good at first light on Jul 29 before you leave Höfn — or skip it for an early night before the big westbound day.

stops.name: Seljavellir Guesthouse
stops.desc: Your booked Höfn stay — a Double Room with Mountain View (this was our top pick, great choice). It's ~10 km northwest of Höfn town, right by tomorrow morning's glacier-lagoon route east.
stops.tip: Free cancellation until Jul 20 — after that it's non-refundable, so lock your plans by then.

title: Glacier Lagoon, Canyons & Puffins to Vík
sub: Work westward from Höfn through the trip's showpieces — icebergs in morning light, hidden canyons, then the black beaches and puffins around Vík right at their evening best.
route: Höfn → Jökulsárlón → Skaftafell → Fjaðrárgljúfur → Vík (~180 mi).

stops.name: Höfn → Jökulsárlón
stops.via: Leave ~8am. A Höfn bakery or your Bónus supplies make an easy breakfast.

stops.name: Jökulsárlón glacier lagoon
stops.desc: House-sized icebergs calve off the glacier and drift across a mirror-still lagoon toward the sea, with seals bobbing between them. Unforgettable — and free.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,000 ISK via the Parka app. Skip the boat tour (~$45pp) — the shoreline is the postcard. Morning means low sun on the bergs and few crowds; walk toward the outlet where they funnel to the ocean.

stops.name: Diamond Beach
stops.desc: Just across Rte 1: chunks of glittering glacier ice strewn across black sand like scattered diamonds. Every piece is different.
stops.tip: Free pull-off parking across Rte 1 — no separate fee from Jökulsárlón. Morning sun backlights the ice — the shot of the trip, and this is exactly why you slept in Höfn. More ice shows near low tide; keep back from the surf.

stops.name: Múlagljúfur canyon (hidden gem)
stops.desc: A gravel track leads to a mossy canyon with waterfalls tumbling toward the glacier — a fraction of Skaftafell's crowds and arguably a better view.
stops.tip: Free gravel roadside parking at the trailhead — no fee, no app. Rough final road — drive slow. Hike to the first viewpoint (30–40 min in) for the money shot, then turn back.
stops.swap: Socked-in or the road's too rough? Skip it and do Svartifoss at Skaftafell instead (below), or give the lagoon extra time.

stops.name: Múlagljúfur → Skaftafell
stops.via: Skirts the Vatnajökull ice cap — glaciers spill down on your right.

stops.name: Lunch at Skaftafell
stops.desc: The visitor-center café is the only real food for miles — soup, sandwiches and veggie plates.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,040 ISK via the Parka app — if you also stop at Jökulsárlón today, both lots give a reciprocal 50% discount.

opts.b: Soup, veggie sandwiches, coffee at the national-park centre.
Eat at the trailhead, then walk up to the basalt-column falls.

stops.name: Svartifoss — the basalt-column waterfall
stops.desc: A slim falls framed by black hexagonal basalt columns — the same shapes that inspired Hallgrímskirkja. ~1.5 km uphill each way from Skaftafell.
stops.tip: Covered by the same Skaftafell parking fee — no extra charge. Pick one Skaftafell-area hike — this or Múlagljúfur earlier — doing both plus the evening at Vík is a lot.

stops.name: Fjaðrárgljúfur canyon
stops.desc: A 100m-deep, 2km serpentine canyon with a river snaking through — walk the rim to a run of jaw-dropping viewpoints.
stops.tip: Parking is 1,000 ISK via the Parka app. Later afternoon is quieter here as the buses thin out. The rim path is easy but muddy after rain; a great viewpoint is only 5 min up if time's tight.

stops.name: Eldhraun moss-covered lava field
stops.desc: A quick roadside stop in one of the largest lava flows in recorded history (the 1783 Laki eruption), now a surreal rolling carpet of grey-green moss.
stops.tip: Free roadside pull-off — no fee. Stay on marked paths — the moss takes decades to regrow and walking on it is illegal. Pull-offs give the best photos.

stops.name: Eldhraun → Vík
stops.via: Fuel in Vík and check tonight's tide time for Reynisfjara before you head to the beaches.

stops.name: Puffins at Dyrhólaey
stops.desc: A dramatic promontory with a lighthouse, a giant sea arch and puffins nesting on the cliffs. Evening is prime — they return from fishing to their burrows.
stops.tip: Parking is 750 ISK via the Parka app. Best puffin odds 6–10pm. The lower area sometimes closes for nesting; the upper cliffs are the reliable spot. Bring a zoom lens.

stops.name: Reynisfjara black-sand beach
stops.desc: Basalt columns, sea stacks and roaring surf on jet-black sand. Evening light is moody and the tour buses are long gone.
stops.tip: Use the upper lot (750 ISK/3 hr via Parka) — cheaper than the lower lot right at the beach (1,000 ISK/3 hr) and just as short a walk down. Deadly sneaker waves — stay 30m back and never turn your back to the sea. Go near low tide (check a tide app for Vík, Jul 29 eve); a reading above 2m is dangerous.

stops.name: Dinner in Vík
stops.desc: Vík punches above its weight for casual food — both of you eat well here, and Suður-Vík covers you both under one roof.

opts.b: Sources fresh, locally caught fish from local producers, plus a highly-praised veggie burger and other vegetarian/vegan options — great historic-house setting with views.
Cozy and cheap; spicy red-lentil & rotating veggie soups with bread.
Brewpub with a great veggie burger — no need to drink to eat well.
Veg-packed bowls, a couple fully vegan; light and warming.

stops.name: Vík Cottages
stops.desc: Your booked Vík night — a 3-star Standard Double/Twin cottage, non-smoking with a large bed, right in Vík and walkable to dinner and the beaches.
stops.gq: Vík Cottages, Klettsvegur 3, Vík í Mýrdal, Iceland

title: Into Reykjavík — Your Last Night
sub: With Vík as your start, you're in the capital by late morning — and since your booked hotel sits right in the old harbour, you get a relaxed afternoon and evening on foot with no onward drive.
route: Vík → Reykjavík, stay the night (~110 mi).

stops.name: Vík → Reykjavík
stops.via: Passes Skógafoss & Seljalandsfoss one last time — wave hello. Leave ~9am; optional coffee stop in Hveragerði or Selfoss.

stops.name: Relaxed lunch in downtown Reykjavík
stops.desc: You're in town early, so take a proper lunch — and you'll still have time to grab dinner here before Keflavík. A few budget legends:
stops.tip: Downtown street parking (P1, red/pink zone) runs ~630 ISK/hr with a 3-hr max, via the EasyPark app. A P2 (blue) zone one block out is ~230 ISK/hr — worth the short walk.

opts.b: Traditional plates + veggie options and skyr, right by the church.
Iceland's famous hot-dog stand — cheap classic (note: meat).
Rich veggie noodle soup — warming and filling.
Food hall — mix stalls, veg + chicken galore.

stops.name: Hallgrímskirkja & rainbow street
stops.desc: Iceland's basalt-inspired church crowns the city. Ride the tower elevator for the best Reykjavík view, then wander down the painted 'rainbow street' (Skólavörðustígur).
stops.tip: Same P1 zone pricing as lunch (~630 ISK/hr via EasyPark). With a half-day here there's no rush — but the tower closes ~5–5:30pm, so don't leave it too late.

stops.name: Harpa, Sun Voyager & the harbour
stops.desc: Stroll the waterfront: the shimmering glass Harpa concert hall (free inside) and the steel Sun Voyager sculpture. With extra time, add the Grandi harbour district or a swim at a geothermal pool.
stops.tip: Harpa has its own paid garage, but it's cheaper to leave the car near your hotel's P2 zone in the old harbour. Your hotel is a ~10-min walk away — drop the car and explore the rest of the day on foot.

stops.name: Dinner in Reykjavík
stops.desc: You're sleeping in the old harbour tonight, so dinner is a short stroll — no driving. Messinn covers both of you under one roof.
stops.tip: Parking near the Marina/Grandi is a P2 zone (~230 ISK/hr) — much cheaper than the P1 downtown core.

opts.b: Beloved local-fish spot (pan-fried plokkfiskur, catch of the day) with solid veggie options too.
Food hall right by your hotel — mix veg + chicken stalls.
Old-harbour café-diner, cheap and local.

stops.name: Reykjavik Marina – Berjaya Hotel
stops.desc: Your final night, right in Reykjavík's old harbour (Grandi) — walkable to Harpa, the Sun Voyager and downtown. B – Double Room; check-in after 3 PM.
stops.gq: Reykjavik Marina Berjaya Hotel, Myrargata 2, Reykjavik, Iceland
stops.tip: Ask at check-in about guest parking — many Reykjavík hotels include it free or discounted for guests. You're ~45 min from KEF — no need to relocate tonight. Just leave early tomorrow.

title: Departure
sub: You're starting from Reykjavík, so build in the 45-minute airport drive. Fuel up, return the car, and fly out.

stops.name: Reykjavík Marina → KEF airport
stops.via: Leave the hotel by ~8:00am to be at the terminal by ~8:45.

stops.name: Return the car & fly home
stops.desc: Flight departs 10:45am. Return your Blue car with a full tank (there's an N1/Olís right by the airport), then check in — aim to be at the terminal by ~8:45am. Grab a last skyr for the road.
stops.tip: Fill up at the last station before the rental return — returning it empty costs far more than airport-adjacent fuel.
